title: What is the CSS content-visibility property?
description: How does content-visibility improve performance?
options: content-visibility: auto skips rendering of off-screen elements (layout, paint, style containment) until they scroll into view; can dramatically improve initial page load performance
correct_answer: content-visibility: auto skips rendering of off-screen elements (layout, paint, style containment) until they scroll into view; can dramatically improve initial page load performance

title: What is the difference between em and rem units?
description: When should you use em vs rem?
options: em is relative to the parent element's font-size (compounds with nesting); rem is relative to the root <html> font-size (consistent throughout). rem is preferred for predictable, accessible sizing
correct_answer: em is relative to the parent element's font-size (compounds with nesting); rem is relative to the root <html> font-size (consistent throughout). rem is preferred for predictable, accessible sizing

title: What is the CSS :focus-visible pseudo-class?
description: How does :focus-visible differ from :focus?
options: :focus-visible applies only when focus should be visually indicated (keyboard navigation), not for mouse clicks; allows removing focus rings for mouse users while keeping accessibility for keyboard users
correct_answer: :focus-visible applies only when focus should be visually indicated (keyboard navigation), not for mouse clicks; allows removing focus rings for mouse users while keeping accessibility for keyboard users

title: What is the prefers-reduced-motion media query?
description: How do you respect motion preferences?
options: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) detects if user has enabled reduced motion in OS settings; use to disable or simplify animations for accessibility
correct_answer: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) detects if user has enabled reduced motion in OS settings; use to disable or simplify animations for accessibility

title: What is the picture element in HTML?
description: How does the <picture> element work?
options: contains multiple elements with media/type attributes and a fallback ; browser picks the first matching source. Used for art direction, format selection (WebP/AVIF), and responsive images
correct_answer: contains multiple elements with media/type attributes and a fallback ; browser picks the first matching source. Used for art direction, format selection (WebP/AVIF), and responsive images

title: What is the CSS aspect-ratio property?
description: How does aspect-ratio work?
options: aspect-ratio: 16/9 sets a preferred aspect ratio; if only width is set, height is calculated automatically. Replaces the padding-top hack for responsive containers
correct_answer: aspect-ratio: 16/9 sets a preferred aspect ratio; if only width is set, height is calculated automatically. Replaces the padding-top hack for responsive containers

title: What is the CSS color-scheme property?
description: How does color-scheme work?
options: color-scheme: light dark tells the browser which color schemes the page supports; browser adjusts default colors for form controls, scrollbars, and system colors accordingly
correct_answer: color-scheme: light dark tells the browser which color schemes the page supports; browser adjusts default colors for form controls, scrollbars, and system colors accordingly

title: Build a responsive image gallery with CSS Grid.
description: Create an auto-fit responsive gallery layout.

title: What is the inert HTML attribute?
description: What does the inert attribute do?
options: inert makes an element and all its descendants non-interactive and invisible to assistive technology; useful for modal backdrops, hidden panels, and freezing sections of the page
correct_answer: inert makes an element and all its descendants non-interactive and invisible to assistive technology; useful for modal backdrops, hidden panels, and freezing sections of the page

title: Build a CSS-only star rating display.
description: Create a visual star rating using CSS.

title: What is the CSS scroll-snap property?
description: How does CSS scroll snapping work?
options: scroll-snap-type on the container defines snapping direction and strictness; scroll-snap-align on children sets alignment points. Creates smooth, native scrolling experiences for carousels and galleries
correct_answer: scroll-snap-type on the container defines snapping direction and strictness; scroll-snap-align on children sets alignment points. Creates smooth, native scrolling experiences for carousels and galleries

title: What is the popover API in HTML?
description: How does the native popover attribute work?
options: popover attribute creates a native popover element with built-in accessibility, keyboard handling, and top-layer rendering; controlled with popovertarget button attribute. Replaces many JS tooltip libraries
correct_answer: popover attribute creates a native popover element with built-in accessibility, keyboard handling, and top-layer rendering; controlled with popovertarget button attribute. Replaces many JS tooltip libraries

title: Build a responsive navigation with CSS flexbox.
description: Create a responsive nav that wraps on small screens.

title: What is the CSS :has() relational pseudo-class?
description: How does :has() work as a parent selector?
options: :has() selects elements that contain matching descendants; e.g., .card:has(img) selects cards containing images, form:has(:invalid) styles forms with invalid fields. The long-awaited 'parent selector'
correct_answer: :has() selects elements that contain matching descendants; e.g., .card:has(img) selects cards containing images, form:has(:invalid) styles forms with invalid fields. The long-awaited 'parent selector'

title: What is the dialog HTML element?
description: How does the native <dialog> element work?
options: <dialog> creates a native modal/non-modal dialog with built-in backdrop, focus trapping, Escape key handling, and accessibility; open with showModal() or show(), close with close() method or form method='dialog'
correct_answer: <dialog> creates a native modal/non-modal dialog with built-in backdrop, focus trapping, Escape key handling, and accessibility; open with showModal() or show(), close with close() method or form method='dialog'

title: Build a CSS-only animated progress bar.
description: Create a progress bar with animation.

title: What is the CSS subgrid feature?
description: How does subgrid work in CSS Grid?
options: grid-template-columns: subgrid allows a nested grid item to adopt its parent grid's tracks; child grid rows/columns align with the parent grid. Enables consistent alignment across nested layouts
correct_answer: grid-template-columns: subgrid allows a nested grid item to adopt its parent grid's tracks; child grid rows/columns align with the parent grid. Enables consistent alignment across nested layouts

title: What is the difference between srcset and sizes attributes?
description: How do srcset and sizes work for responsive images?
options: srcset lists image sources with descriptors (widths or pixel density); sizes tells the browser the layout width at breakpoints so it picks the best source. Together they enable automatic responsive image selection
correct_answer: srcset lists image sources with descriptors (widths or pixel density); sizes tells the browser the layout width at breakpoints so it picks the best source. Together they enable automatic responsive image selection

title: Build a CSS-only toggle switch.
description: Create an accessible toggle switch without JavaScript.

title: What is the CSS accent-color property?
description: How does accent-color style form controls?
options: accent-color sets the accent color for form controls (checkboxes, radio buttons, range sliders, progress bars) with a single property; the browser automatically handles contrast and states
correct_answer: accent-color sets the accent color for form controls (checkboxes, radio buttons, range sliders, progress bars) with a single property; the browser automatically handles contrast and states
